mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (accuracy < 52%), or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle periods, or the predictive power reverses sign with positive returns following supply contractions.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.5% constrains liquidity and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >58% of high-rate periods, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns (accuracy < 50%), or the relationship breaks down across multiple consecutive quarters in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading below 90.0 indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain stress, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 90.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances, or the threshold loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.
